stress_target: MP-critical definition (defines the deontic duty-bearer)

definition.value: the natural or legal person, public authority, agency or other body which, alone or jointly with others, determines the purposes and means of the processing of personal data; where the purposes and means of such processing are determined by Union or Member State law, the controller or the specific criteria for its nomination may be provided for by Union or Member State law

classification_rationale: Foundational definition of the controller role.
anchor: 'controller' means the natural or legal person ... which ... determines the purposes and means of the processing of personal data

stress_target: MP-critical definition (Art 6(1)(a)/9(2)(a) consent)

definition.value: any freely given, specific, informed and unambiguous indication of the data subject's wishes by which he or she, by a statement or by a clear affirmative action, signifies agreement to the processing of personal data relating to him or her

classification_rationale: Foundational definition; consent is MigrainePredict's lawful basis.
anchor: 'consent' of the data subject means any freely given, specific, informed and unambiguous indication of the data subject's wishes

stress_target: lawfulness gate ('shall be lawful only if') — deontic-vs-applicability boundary [JUDGMENT CALL]

condition.value: the data subject has given consent to the processing of his or her personal data for one or more specific purposes

classification_rationale: Lawful-basis gate read as a conditional PERMISSION: processing is permitted where consent is given. JUDGMENT CALL: 'Processing shall be lawful only if' could alternatively be MATERIAL applicability — confirm convention.
anchor: Processing shall be lawful only if ... (a) the data subject has given consent to the processing of his or her personal data for one or more specific purposes

stress_target: multi-point enumeration sibling (Art 9(2)(a)); MP's actual Art 9 basis

object.value: special categories of personal data referred to in paragraph 1

condition.value: the data subject has given explicit consent to the processing of those personal data for one or more specified purposes, except where Union or Member State law provide that the prohibition referred to in paragraph 1 may not be lifted by the data subject

classification_rationale: Exception to the Art 9(1) PROHIBITION via explicit consent => PERMISSION. Parent in references.
anchor: the data subject has given explicit consent to the processing of those personal data for one or more specified purposes

stress_target: profile-gate precision probe: special-category keyword present but NOT MP's basis

object.value: special categories of personal data referred to in paragraph 1

condition.value: processing is necessary for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes in accordance with Article 89(1) based on Union or Member State law which shall be proportionate to the aim pursued

classification_rationale: Exception to Art 9(1) for research => PERMISSION. PROBE: touches the special-category keyword (gate likely matches data_categories -> hc=True), but research is NOT MigrainePredict's operative basis, so the correct hc is FALSE. Tests whether the keyword gate over-matches.
anchor: processing is necessary for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research purposes or statistical purposes in accordance with Article 89(1)

stress_target: active named subject: 'The controller shall' (subject method STATED, not CONTEXT)

predicate.value: take appropriate measures to provide information and communication relating to processing in a concise, transparent, intelligible and easily accessible form, using clear and plain language

object.value: any information referred to in Articles 13 and 14 and any communication under Articles 15 to 22 and 34

classification_rationale: Transparency obligation with an EXPLICITLY NAMED subject — tests subject method STATED vs the passive-voice CONTEXT cases in the dev set.
anchor: The controller shall take appropriate measures to provide any information referred to in Articles 13 and 14 ... to the data subject in a concise, transparent, intelligible and easily accessible form

stress_target: data-subject RIGHT reframed as controller PROHIBITION [JUDGMENT CALL]; MP-relevant (automated decisions)

predicate.value: subject to a decision based solely on automated processing, including profiling

condition.value: which produces legal effects concerning him or her or similarly significantly affects him or her

classification_rationale: Framed in the text as a data-subject RIGHT; per the duty-bearer convention it is reframed as a controller PROHIBITION with the data subject as beneficiary. JUDGMENT CALL: confirm rights are reframed this way (vs. modelled as a right held by the subject).
anchor: The data subject shall have the right not to be subject to a decision based solely on automated processing, including profiling, which produces legal effects concerning him or her

stress_target: DISPENSATION: exception to an OBLIGATION (Art 30(1)/(2)) — modality branch absent from dev set

predicate.value: maintain a record of processing activities

object.value: the obligations referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2

condition.value: an enterprise or an organisation employing fewer than 250 persons unless the processing it carries out is likely to result in a risk to the rights and freedoms of data subjects, the processing is not occasional, or the processing includes special categories of data as referred to in Article 9(1) or personal data relating to criminal convictions and offences

classification_rationale: Exception to the record-keeping OBLIGATION in Art 30(1)/(2) => DISPENSATION (NOT permission). Tests the obligation-exception branch of the modality rule. NOTE: the 'unless ... special categories' clause means a health-data controller like MigrainePredict likely CANNOT rely on this dispensation. [GOLD FIX: added gdpr:art_10 cross-ref ('referred to in Article 10').]
anchor: The obligations referred to in paragraphs 1 and 2 shall not apply to an enterprise or an organisation employing fewer than 250 persons unless ... the processing includes special categories of data

stress_target: temporal qualifier ('72 hours') inside an OBLIGATION (distinct from TEMPORAL scope)

predicate.value: notify the personal data breach to the supervisory authority without undue delay and, where feasible, not later than 72 hours after having become aware of it

condition.value: unless the personal data breach is unlikely to result in a risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons

classification_rationale: Breach-notification obligation; the '72 hours' is a temporal QUALIFIER within an obligation, which must NOT be misread as TEMPORAL applicability scope.
anchor: the controller shall without undue delay and, where feasible, not later than 72 hours after having become aware of it, notify the personal data breach to the supervisory authority

stress_target: conditional obligation gated on 'high risk' (MP is high-risk -> DPIA required)

predicate.value: carry out an assessment of the impact of the envisaged processing operations on the protection of personal data, prior to the processing

condition.value: where a type of processing in particular using new technologies, and taking into account the nature, scope, context and purposes of the processing, is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons

classification_rationale: DPIA obligation triggered by likely high risk; directly engaged for MigrainePredict.
anchor: Where a type of processing ... is likely to result in a high risk ... the controller shall, prior to the processing, carry out an assessment of the impact

stress_target: AI Act deontic (HITL); AI-Act duty-bearer is 'the provider', NOT 'the controller' [JUDGMENT CALL]

predicate.value: design and develop in such a way, including with appropriate human-machine interface tools, that they can be effectively overseen by natural persons during the period in which they are in use

classification_rationale: Human-oversight obligation on high-risk AI systems. JUDGMENT CALL / NEW WRINKLE: AI Act duty-bearers are 'provider'/'deployer', not GDPR's 'controller' — the subject convention needs an AI-Act variant. Here the duty-bearer is the provider.
anchor: High-risk AI systems shall be designed and developed in such a way ... that they can be effectively overseen by natural persons during the period in which they are in use

stress_target: per-area Annex III extraction (not the chapeau); profile-gate precision probe [JUDGMENT CALL]

condition.value: in so far as their use is permitted under relevant Union or national law: (a) remote biometric identification systems; (b) AI systems intended to be used for biometric categorisation according to sensitive or protected attributes; (c) AI systems intended to be used for emotion recognition

classification_rationale: Annex III Area 1 as a per-area record. PROBE: contains the 'biometric' keyword (gate likely matches), but MigrainePredict's high-risk basis is the Art 6(1) MEDICAL-DEVICE route, not standalone biometric identification/categorisation/emotion use — so the correct hc is FALSE. Tests whether the gate distinguishes keyword presence from MP's operative high-risk basis. (Flagged for domain confirmation.) [GOLD FIX: applies_to set to the source heading 'biometrics'.]
anchor: Biometrics, in so far as their use is permitted under relevant Union or national law: (a) remote biometric identification systems

stress_target: second deontic statement in a paragraph (Art 12(1) oral provision) — gold completeness

condition.value: when requested by the data subject, provided that the identity of the data subject is proven by other means

classification_rationale: Second statement in Art 12(1): a controller-addressed PERMISSION to provide information orally, distinct from the H06 obligation. Tests multi-statement-per-paragraph capture.
anchor: When requested by the data subject, the information may be provided orally, provided that the identity of the data subject is proven by other means

stress_target: second deontic statement in a paragraph (Art 35(1) single assessment) — gold completeness

object.value: a set of similar processing operations that present similar high risks

classification_rationale: Second statement in Art 35(1): a controller-addressed PERMISSION to batch similar high-risk operations into one DPIA, distinct from the H10 obligation.
anchor: A single assessment may address a set of similar processing operations that present similar high risks

stress_target: rights-reframing CONTROL: simple positive right, no stated duty -> controller OBLIGATION

object.value: inaccurate personal data concerning the data subject

classification_rationale: Data-subject right with no explicit controller duty -> reframed to the correlative controller OBLIGATION. subject=controller, beneficiary=data subject. Modality follows the effect (provide rectification -> OBLIGATION).
anchor: The data subject shall have the right to obtain from the controller without undue delay the rectification of inaccurate personal data concerning him or her

stress_target: positive right + grounds; controller duty STATED ('shall have the obligation to erase') -> OBLIGATION; anchor-on-stated-duty path

object.value: personal data concerning the data subject

condition.value: where one of the following grounds applies: the personal data are no longer necessary in relation to the purposes for which they were collected or otherwise processed; the data subject withdraws the consent on which the processing is based and there is no other legal ground for the processing; the data subject objects and there are no overriding legitimate grounds; the personal data have been unlawfully processed; erasure is required for compliance with a legal obligation

classification_rationale: Erasure: the controller duty is STATED ('the controller shall have the obligation to erase'), so anchor on it directly rather than reframe. OBLIGATION conditioned on the listed grounds.
anchor: the controller shall have the obligation to erase personal data without undue delay where one of the following grounds applies

stress_target: exception to an OBLIGATION (Art 17(1)/(2)) -> DISPENSATION; tests condition+exception survive the rights cluster

condition.value: to the extent that processing is necessary for exercising the right of freedom of expression and information; for compliance with a legal obligation or a public-interest task; for reasons of public interest in the area of public health; for archiving purposes in the public interest, scientific or historical research or statistical purposes; or for the establishment, exercise or defence of legal claims

classification_rationale: Exception to the erasure OBLIGATION ('Paragraphs 1 and 2 shall not apply...') -> DISPENSATION (release from a duty, not a permission-from-prohibition). Parent obligations in references.
anchor: Paragraphs 1 and 2 shall not apply to the extent that processing is necessary

stress_target: EFFECT-NOT-GRAMMAR edge: 'right to object' (positive form) -> controller PROHIBITION ('shall no longer process'); duty STATED

condition.value: where the data subject objects, on grounds relating to his or her particular situation, to processing based on point (e) or (f) of Article 6(1), including profiling; unless the controller demonstrates compelling legitimate grounds which override the interests, rights and freedoms of the data subject, or for the establishment, exercise or defence of legal claims

classification_rationale: 'Right to object' is grammatically positive but its correlative duty is a PROHIBITION ('the controller shall no longer process'). Modality follows the effect, not the grammar. JUDGMENT CALL: the 'unless compelling legitimate grounds' clause is folded into condition here; it could alternatively be a separate PERMISSION carve-out (exception to this prohibition).
anchor: The controller shall no longer process the personal data unless the controller demonstrates compelling legitimate grounds for the processing

stress_target: blocked second-statement (Art 12(1) 'provided in writing'); distinct from H06 obligation + H15 permission; unblocked by statement-id

predicate.value: be provided in writing, or by other means, including, where appropriate, by electronic means

classification_rationale: Third statement in Art 12(1): a controller OBLIGATION on the FORM of provision (in writing/electronic), distinct from the H06 transparency obligation and the H15 oral-provision permission.
anchor: The information shall be provided in writing, or by other means, including, where appropriate, by electronic means

stress_target: blocked second-statement (Art 16(1) completion); same-modality collision with H17 rectification; unblocked by statement-id

predicate.value: complete, including by means of providing a supplementary statement

condition.value: taking into account the purposes of the processing

classification_rationale: Second right in Art 16 (completion of incomplete data) reframed to the correlative controller OBLIGATION. Distinct from H17 (rectification of inaccurate data); both are (art_16, DEONTIC, OBLIGATION).
anchor: the data subject shall have the right to have incomplete personal data completed, including by means of providing a supplementary statement

stress_target: blocked second-statement (Art 33(1) reasons-for-delay); same-modality collision with H09 notify; unblocked by statement-id

predicate.value: be accompanied by reasons for the delay

object.value: the notification to the supervisory authority

condition.value: where the notification to the supervisory authority is not made within 72 hours

classification_rationale: Second statement in Art 33(1): a controller OBLIGATION to give reasons for a late breach notification, distinct from the H09 notify obligation; both are (art_33, DEONTIC, OBLIGATION).
anchor: Where the notification to the supervisory authority is not made within 72 hours, it shall be accompanied by reasons for the delay

stress_target: MEASURES enumeration: chapeau OBLIGATION whose sub-points (a)-(d) are technical MEASURES (objects/content), NOT condition-grounds — counter-canary to Art 5(1) for the structural-inheritance gate (must NOT inherit-and-route-to-condition)

predicate.value: implement appropriate technical and organisational measures

object.value: a level of security appropriate to the risk

condition.value: Taking into account the state of the art, the costs of implementation and the nature, scope, context and purposes of processing as well as the risk of varying likelihood and severity for the rights and freedoms of natural persons

classification_rationale: Security-of-processing OBLIGATION. The enumerated points (a)-(d) (pseudonymisation/encryption, confidentiality/integrity, restore-availability, testing) are MEASURES the obligation requires, i.e. object content, not conditions that trigger it. Structural-inheritance gating must spare this paragraph.
anchor: the controller and the processor shall implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to ensure a level of security appropriate to the risk, including inter alia as appropriate: (a) the pseudonymisation and encryption of personal data

stress_target: OBLIGATION contrast to H09 (Art 33(1)): same breach-communication act, but beneficiary = the data subject (not the supervisory authority) and gated on 'likely to result in a high risk' rather than the 72h / unless-unlikely frame

condition.value: the personal data breach is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons

classification_rationale: Breach-communication OBLIGATION on the controller toward the data subject, triggered by a high-risk breach. Beneficiary (data subject) and the high-risk gate are the discriminating fields vs Art 33(1).
anchor: When the personal data breach is likely to result in a high risk to the rights and freedoms of natural persons, the controller shall communicate the personal data breach to the data subject without undue delay

stress_target: DISPENSATION at paragraph level exempting an OBLIGATION in OTHER paragraphs (Art 13(1)/(2)/(3)); predicate in normalized POSITIVE form ('provide') with DISPENSATION modality carrying the exemption; references = the exempted paragraphs

object.value: the information referred to in paragraphs 1, 2 and 3

condition.value: the data subject already has the information

classification_rationale: 'Paragraphs 1, 2 and 3 shall not apply where...' = exception to an OBLIGATION -> DISPENSATION. Exempted action (provide information) inherited in positive form; exemption carried by modality, not by a negated predicate.
anchor: Paragraphs 1, 2 and 3 shall not apply where and insofar as the data subject already has the information

stress_target: MATERIAL scope_type (non-temporal applicability) — restores APPLICABILITY coverage after H11/H14 removal; INCLUDES polarity; scope qualifier split into condition (applies_to = the WHAT, condition = the automated-means/filing-system HOW)

condition.value: wholly or partly by automated means and to the processing other than by automated means of personal data which form part of a filing system or are intended to form part of a filing system

classification_rationale: Material scope clause ('This Regulation applies to the processing of personal data...'): unconditional INCLUDES applicability, scope_type MATERIAL. applies_to is the processing itself; the automated-means / filing-system qualifier is the scope condition. Covers MigrainePredict's automated processing.
anchor: This Regulation applies to the processing of personal data wholly or partly by automated means and to the processing other than by automated means of personal data which form part of a filing system

stress_target: OBLIGATION introducing the controller->processor relationship (not previously in holdout); condition is the genuine trigger ('Where processing is to be carried out on behalf of a controller'), not the manner clause; MP-relevant (Skein/OPORA as processors)

object.value: processors providing sufficient guarantees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures

condition.value: Where processing is to be carried out on behalf of a controller

classification_rationale: Processor-selection OBLIGATION on the controller, triggered where processing is carried out by a processor on the controller's behalf. The 'in such a manner that...' clause is purpose/manner content on the guarantees, not the triggering condition.
anchor: Where processing is to be carried out on behalf of a controller ... the controller shall use only processors providing sufficient guarantees to implement appropriate technical and organisational measures

stress_target: AI-Act high-risk OBLIGATION core to MigrainePredict (risk management system); AI-Act duty-bearer = 'the provider'. [VERIFY verbatim predicate/condition against AI-Act corpus text before scoring]

condition.value: in relation to high-risk AI systems

classification_rationale: Risk-management-system OBLIGATION for high-risk AI; MigrainePredict is high-risk via Art 6(1), so this is a core applicable duty. Duty-bearer 'the provider' per AI-Act convention (cf. H12).
anchor: A risk management system shall be established, implemented, documented and maintained in relation to high-risk AI systems
